task:
Draft a new founder-approved ADR that makes `run-tests` a REQUIRED CHECKPOINT for code-touching atoms. This atom writes ONE file only: the ADR. Do NOT touch runtime, prompts, or tests yet — enforcement wiring is a later atom.

FILE TO CREATE: `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md` (0046 is the next free number; 0045 is the current max). Match the house ADR format exactly — read `cocoder/decisions/0023-workspace-commit-spine.md`, `cocoder/decisions/0033-testing-as-a-play-capability.md`, and `cocoder/decisions/0028-play-taxonomy-three-axes.md` first to copy the structure: `# ADR-0046 — <title>` heading, a Status block with relationship lines (Status / Builds on / Reconciles / Amends), then `## Context`, `## Decision` (numbered sub-sections), `## Consequences`, and a `## Conflict audit (per ADR-0014, for the founder)` section.

DECISION TO RECORD (this is founder-approved — record it, do not re-litigate it):
Make testing STRUCTURAL, not cultural. An atom that changes product code (e.g. `packages/**`) cannot pass the verify gate without a green `run-tests` result. The requirement is inherited by this and every future/onboarded workspace via the base persona/standards surface (`packages/personas/base/**`), NOT dependent on Oscar's per-atom discretion or a repo having CI.

ARCHITECTURE SEAM — Option A, the founder-approved one (state it explicitly and explain WHY it reconciles the prior ADRs):
- The deterministic `run-tests` result feeds the SINGLE EXISTING verify gate (ADR-0013 / ADR-0023 §3) as a REQUIRED INPUT. Oscar still issues the pass/fail verdict.
- It is NOT a second independent commit gate. Do NOT introduce `requiredCheckpoints` / `runCommitGate` as a parallel lane — that would contradict ADR-0023's one-self-clearing-gate principle. Reuse the existing `execCriterion` deterministic exec path; do not fork a second test runner.

RECONCILE these four ADRs explicitly in the Decision and/or Conflict-audit sections (cite each by number, say what changes and what does NOT):
- ADR-0013 (orchestration-observation: the per-atom verify gate) — the gate gains a required `run-tests`-green precondition for code atoms; the deterministic 'no pass, no commit' machine boundary is unchanged; Oscar's judgment over the diff is still unconstrained.
- ADR-0023 (workspace commit spine: ONE self-clearing verify gate before the spine commits, scope advisory, no second commit lane) — this ADR adds a required INPUT to that one gate, it does NOT add a second lane; the gate stays automated and self-clearing and never parks awaiting a human.
- ADR-0033 (testing is a Play capability) — reuse the existing `run-tests` Play (all-persona, persona-requested); do not create a new persona or a forked runner.
- ADR-0028 (Play taxonomy three axes) — `run-tests` keeps its current taxonomy (`triggerClass: persona-requested`, `executionModel: hybrid`, `writeScope: []`); no enum/schema change.

ESCAPE HATCH (must be in the Decision — this is part of the founder-approved Objective): a repo or atom with genuinely NO test surface (a docs-only change, or a brand-new repo with no tests) must DEGRADE TO ADVISORY + FLAG, never a hard block of legitimate work. Define the trigger precisely (code-touching atom with a discoverable test command present => required-green; no discoverable test surface => advisory-flag). The checkpoint binds AGENTS and must NEVER block the founder's own direct work (ADR-0029 spirit / founder-vs-agent boundary).

VERIFIED-WHEN for the ADR itself (so the Decision is testable downstream): (1) a code atom cannot commit without a green run-tests result; (2) the requirement is inherited by onboarded repos via a base standard / required-checkpoint binding, not a per-repo opt-in; (3) the deterministic exec path (`execCriterion`) is reused, not forked; (4) behavior-pinning suites + `scripts/proof-*.mjs` stay green; (5) the advisory-degrade escape exists for no-test-surface cases.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA for this atom: the single new file `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md` exists, follows the house ADR format, names and reconciles all four ADRs above, states Option A as the chosen seam with rationale, and includes the escape hatch and the verified-when list. MUST NOT: edit any other file; change any runtime, prompt, schema, or test; alter the `run-tests` Play manifest. No code is touched in this atom, so no test run is required for it — but do `git status`/list to confirm only that one file changed.

Add the portable REQUIRED-TEST-CHECKPOINT contract to the cross-persona standard. This atom edits ONE file: `packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md`. Do NOT touch runtime code, the run-tests Play, or tests in this atom — the runtime enforcement is a separate later atom. This atom authors the portable contract that ADR-0046 just decided; read `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md` first so your wording matches the decision.

WHY HERE: `shared-standards.md` is prepended to every persona prompt and is inherited by every onboarded workspace, so putting the contract here is what makes the requirement portable (Objective verified-when #2: inherited by onboarded repos, not a per-repo opt-in). It is also ADR-0012 portability-tested, so the text must teach the rule with repo-specific nouns STRIPPED OUT.

WHAT TO ADD: a new short section (suggested heading `## Required Test Checkpoint For Code Changes`, placed near the existing Evidence section since it is an evidence rule). Keep it tight and role-neutral — a few sentences, not a treatise. It must state:
- A change that touches product or machinery code must carry a GREEN result from the repo's test Play (the deterministic test command) as a REQUIRED INPUT to the single existing verify gate before that change can pass and commit. Testing is structural, not discretionary — an agent cannot skip it by choice.
- This is a required INPUT to the ONE self-clearing verify gate, NOT a second commit gate or a parallel lane (cite ADR-0046; do not invent `requiredCheckpoints`).
- ESCAPE: when a change has no discoverable test surface (e.g. a docs-only change, or a brand-new repo with no test command yet), the checkpoint DEGRADES to advisory + flag — the work may still proceed, but the closeout must surface that no runnable test surface was found. It must never hard-block legitimate setup or docs work.
- It binds AGENTS and runner-managed work; it never blocks the founder's own direct edits or recovery work.
- Cite ADR-0046 as the owner of this checkpoint.

PORTABILITY (ADR-0012 — hard requirement, a base-personas test enforces it): write the rule so it still teaches with this repo's nouns stripped. Do NOT name implementation specifics like `execCriterion`, `pnpm -r test`, `run-tests-preflight.mjs`, or `packages/**` paths in `shared-standards.md` — say 'the test Play', 'the deterministic test command', 'product or machinery code' generically. Implementation nouns stay out of the shared standard.

MUST NOT BREAK: `base-personas.test.ts` pins several existing sections of `shared-standards.md` verbatim (owner-mapped durable orchestration changes, the elegance standard, launch-smoke/artifact evidence, design-seam prose). Do not reword, reorder, or delete any existing pinned content — ADD your new section without disturbing what is already there.

EXIT CRITERION (this is itself a code-touching atom under packages/**, so run the test): run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/personas test` and confirm it is green; paste the command, exit code, and the suite summary as your evidence. Also `git status --porcelain` to confirm ONLY `packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md` changed.

ACCEPTANCE: exactly one file changed (`shared-standards.md`); a new short portable required-test-checkpoint section is present stating the required-input/Option-A framing, the advisory no-test-surface escape, the agent-not-founder boundary, and the ADR-0046 citation; the personas test suite is green; no existing pinned section was altered.

Implement the STRUCTURAL runtime enforcement for ADR-0046 (read `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md` first). This is the atom that turns the required-test-checkpoint from a written standard into a machine boundary. It is itself a code-touching atom, so it MUST ship green tests.

SEAM (already mapped — implement HERE, do not invent a new gate): in `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts`, the verify→commit logic is the `verdict.verdict === 'pass'` branch (~lines 490-508) which calls `runCommitGate(...)` exactly once. The precedent to mirror is the loop-criterion rerun at ~line 400, which runs `execCriterion(directive.loop.criterion, worktreePath)` deterministically before verify. `execCriterion` is already a threaded dependency of `executeAgentStep` (input field `execCriterion`).

WHAT TO BUILD — Option A, a REQUIRED INPUT to the ONE existing gate (NOT a second gate/lane):
When Oscar's verdict is `pass`, BEFORE calling `runCommitGate`, run the required test checkpoint for CODE-TOUCHING atoms:
1. Determine if the atom is code-touching: it changed at least one product/machinery SOURCE file (e.g. under `packages/**` / source globs), as opposed to a docs-only or governance-only atom (only `docs/**`, `*.md`, `cocoder/**`, ADRs, etc.). Compute this from the atom's changed file set relative to `headBefore` (the same snapshot the quarantine/verify logic already reasons about). A docs/governance-only atom gets NO hard checkpoint (ADR-0046 §1.3) — proceed exactly as today.
2. If code-touching, resolve the repo's deterministic test command from the existing `run-tests` Play capability (ADR-0033) — its `deterministicStep` (e.g. `scripts/checks/run-tests-preflight.mjs`). REUSE `execCriterion` to run it in `worktreePath`. Do NOT fork a second test runner, and do NOT shell out a new bespoke path.
   - GREEN (exit 0): proceed to the existing `runCommitGate` call unchanged. Record a `required-checkpoint-green` run event with the command + exit code.
   - RED (non-zero): the atom does NOT commit. Treat it like a rejected/quarantined atom: do NOT call `runCommitGate`, call `quarantineAtom(atomIndex, headBefore, ...)`, set the work item abandoned, record a `required-checkpoint-red` event with command + exit code + output tail, and set an outcome line like `atom N BLOCKED by required test checkpoint (run-tests red) — nothing committed`. Oscar's pass is necessary but NOT sufficient for a code atom.
   - NO DISCOVERABLE TEST SURFACE (the run-tests deterministic step is absent/unresolvable for this workspace): DEGRADE TO ADVISORY — record a `required-checkpoint-advisory-no-test-surface` event/flag and PROCEED to commit. Never hard-block legitimate work (ADR-0046 escape hatch). Distinguish this from a red result: a command that ran and failed is RED (block); a command that does not exist / cannot be resolved is advisory (proceed + flag).
3. This path governs only runner-managed AGENT atoms (Bob's work through the verify gate). It must not, and structurally does not, touch the founder's own direct edits — keep it that way; do not add any founder-path test gating.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- Still EXACTLY ONE `runCommitGate` call and one commit per atom. Do NOT introduce a `requiredCheckpoints` array, a second `runCommitGate`, a `runCommitGate`-parallel lane, or a new commit path — that contradicts ADR-0023's one-self-clearing-gate principle and ADR-0046 §2. The checkpoint is a precondition INPUT to the single gate.
- Reuse `execCriterion` and the `run-tests` Play deterministic step. No forked runner.
- Preserve all existing behavior for docs/governance-only atoms and for the loop path.

TESTS (REQUIRED — this atom cannot pass without them; put them with the existing runner/agent-step tests, e.g. `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` or a new `required-checkpoint.test.ts`): behavior-pin all five cases — (a) code-touching atom + GREEN test → commits; (b) code-touching atom + RED test → NOT committed, quarantined, event recorded; (c) code-touching atom + NO test surface → advisory flag event + commits; (d) docs/governance-only atom → no checkpoint runs, commits as today; (e) the checkpoint only fires on a passing Oscar verdict for a code atom (a fail verdict path is unchanged). Use the existing test harness's injectable `execCriterion` to simulate green/red/absent.

EXIT CRITERION: run the FULL core suite `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` (or the repo typecheck) — both green. Paste the command, exit code, and suite summary. Then `git status --porcelain` — expected changes are confined to `packages/core/src/runner/**` and `packages/core/tests/**` (and only those). If you find the test command must be threaded from `runner.ts` into `executeAgentStep`, that is in scope (runner.ts), but keep the change minimal and explain it.

ACCEPTANCE: code-touching atoms cannot commit without a green run-tests result; red blocks + quarantines; no-test-surface degrades to advisory + flag and still commits; docs-only atoms are unaffected; exactly one runCommitGate call remains (no second lane / no requiredCheckpoints); execCriterion + the run-tests Play are reused; the five behavior-pinning tests exist and the full core suite + typecheck are green.

Re-implement the STRUCTURAL runtime enforcement for ADR-0046 (read `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md`). Your previous attempt was correct and green EXCEPT the code-touching predicate was too narrow and was rejected; the working tree was reverted, so re-apply the full change with the predicate FIXED. It is itself a code-touching atom, so it MUST ship green tests.

THE FIX (the reason for rejection): the predicate must NOT restrict code-touching to `packages/**`/`scripts/**`. That makes the checkpoint a per-layout opt-in, so an onboarded repo with source under `src/`, `app/`, `lib/`, or the repo root silently gets NO checkpoint — which defeats the priority's cross-repo durability purpose (verified-when #2) and the ADR's 'product or machinery code' scope. INVERT the default:
- A file is CODE-TOUCHING if it has a source extension (`.ts .tsx .js .jsx .mjs .cjs .mts .cts`) AND is NOT under an excluded governance/docs path.
- EXCLUDE (not code-touching): paths under `docs/` and `cocoder/` (governance + portable run history), and any `*.md`/`*.mdx` file.
- Therefore a source-extension file ANYWHERE ELSE — `packages/`, `scripts/`, `src/`, `app/`, `lib/`, repo root — IS code-touching. Non-source extensions are not code-touching.

SEAM (unchanged from before, implement HERE — do not invent a new gate): in `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts`, the `verdict.verdict === 'pass'` branch (~lines 490-508) calls `runCommitGate(...)` exactly once. Mirror the loop-criterion rerun precedent (~line 400) which runs `execCriterion(...)` deterministically. `execCriterion` is already a threaded dependency. Thread the repo's deterministic test command from the `run-tests` Play's `deterministicStep` via `runner.ts` (as before: `effectivePlays.find(p => p.id === 'run-tests')?.deterministicStep?.ref ?? null`).

BEHAVIOR — Option A, a REQUIRED INPUT to the ONE existing gate (NOT a second gate/lane):
When Oscar's verdict is `pass`, BEFORE `runCommitGate`, for a CODE-TOUCHING atom (per the fixed predicate, computed from the atom's changed files relative to `headBefore`):
- Resolve the run-tests deterministic command. If absent/unresolvable/escapes-root/not-found in this workspace => NO test surface: record a `required-checkpoint-advisory-no-test-surface` event and PROCEED to commit (never hard-block). Distinguish absent (advisory) from a command that RAN and failed (red, block).
- Else run it via `execCriterion` in `worktreePath`: GREEN (exit 0) => record `required-checkpoint-green`, proceed to the single `runCommitGate` unchanged. RED (non-zero) => do NOT commit: record `required-checkpoint-red` (command, exitCode, output tail), `quarantineAtom(atomIndex, headBefore, ...)`, set work item abandoned, set an outcome line like `atom N BLOCKED by required test checkpoint (run-tests red) — nothing committed`, and return `{ kind: 'blocked', outcomeLine }`.
- A docs/governance-only atom (no code-touching file) runs NO checkpoint — proceed exactly as today.
- Keep the `changedFiles` snapshot frozen for the commit gate (as your prior attempt did) so test side-effects are not swept into the commit.

HARD CONSTRAINTS: still EXACTLY ONE `runCommitGate` call and one commit per atom; NO `requiredCheckpoints` array, no second gate/lane (ADR-0023 / ADR-0046 §2). Reuse `execCriterion` + the run-tests Play deterministic step; no forked runner. This governs only runner-managed AGENT atoms; never gate the founder's direct work.

TESTS (REQUIRED — re-add your prior five behavior pins in `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`, PLUS one new pin for the fix): (a) code-touching `packages/**` + GREEN => commits; (b) code-touching + RED => quarantined, not committed, event; (c) code-touching + NO test surface => advisory flag + commits, execCriterion not called; (d) docs-only (`docs/*.md`) => no checkpoint, commits; (e) checkpoint only fires after a passing verdict (fail path unchanged); (f) NEW — an atom changing a NON-packages source file (e.g. `src/feature.ts`) IS treated as code-touching and runs the checkpoint (proves cross-repo inheritance). Also pin that a non-source file outside packages (e.g. a root `config.json` or `README.md`) is NOT code-touching.

EXIT CRITERION: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` both green — paste command, exit code, summary. `git status --porcelain` confined to `packages/core/src/runner/**` and `packages/core/tests/**`.

ACCEPTANCE: source files in ANY layout (not just packages/scripts) trigger the checkpoint; docs/governance excluded; red blocks+quarantines; no-surface degrades to advisory+commits; exactly one runCommitGate (no second lane); execCriterion + run-tests Play reused; the six behavior pins exist and the full core suite + typecheck are green.

Author the runnable archive-proof harness for the required-test-checkpoint (ADR-0046), satisfying the priority's verified-when #4 ('behavior-pinning suites + scripts/proof-*.mjs stay green'). This atom writes ONE file: `scripts/proof-required-checkpoint.mjs`.

FOLLOW THE EXISTING CONVENTION EXACTLY — read `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` first and mirror its shape: a single command (`node scripts/proof-required-checkpoint.mjs`) that runs the REAL behavior-pinning tests (it must NOT reimplement or re-stub the behavior) and prints a PASS/FAIL table mapping each ADR-0046 'Verified when' clause to the actual named test that proves it. It must exit NON-ZERO if any row is red OR if any mapped clause has no matching passing test (a renamed/deleted test must turn the proof red, never silently pass — copy proof-direct-spine's loud-fail behavior).

WHAT TO RUN (the real suites already committed):
- `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` — the six required-checkpoint behavior pins (their names contain phrases like 'required test checkpoint', 'no discoverable test surface', 'source files outside packages trigger the required checkpoint', 'docs-only atom does not run the required test checkpoint'). Run the suite via the same mechanism proof-direct-spine uses (pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test, capturing per-test pass/fail). NOTE: not every pin contains the word 'checkpoint' (e.g. the advisory one says 'no discoverable test surface'), so do NOT rely on a single -t substring that silently drops a pin — anchor each clause to a substring that actually and uniquely matches its test, and fail loudly if a clause's anchor matches zero passing tests.
- `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts` — proves the portable required-checkpoint standard lives in shared-standards.md and stays portable (ADR-0012). Map this to verified-when #2 (inherited by onboarded repos).

MAP THESE ADR-0046 'Verified when' CLAUSES, each to a real passing test:
1. A code atom cannot commit without a green run-tests result -> the green-commits pin AND the red-blocks-and-quarantines pin.
2. Inherited by onboarded repos, not a per-repo opt-in -> the 'source files outside packages trigger the required checkpoint' pin AND the personas portability suite.
3. The deterministic exec path (execCriterion) is reused, not forked -> the green pin that asserts the run-tests command was run via the injected execCriterion (command + cwd).
4. Behavior-pinning suites + proof script stay green -> the suite running green here (this proof's own green table is the evidence).
5. Advisory-degrade escape for no-test-surface -> the 'no discoverable test surface records advisory flag and commits' pin.
Also include the docs-only-no-checkpoint and fail-verdict-no-checkpoint pins as supporting rows.

HEADER COMMENT: like proof-direct-spine, open with a short comment block naming ADR-0046, the one command to run, what a green table means (the required test checkpoint is structurally enforced — code atoms can't commit without green tests, inherited across repos, with an advisory escape), and what a red row means (a specific named failing test).

EXIT CRITERION: run `node scripts/proof-required-checkpoint.mjs` and confirm it prints a fully GREEN table and exits 0 — paste the table and exit code. Also confirm `git status --porcelain` shows ONLY `scripts/proof-required-checkpoint.mjs` added. (This .mjs file is itself code-touching under the new predicate, so expect the runner's own required checkpoint to run pnpm -r test when this atom commits — the whole monorepo must stay green; do not break any package.)

ACCEPTANCE: one new file `scripts/proof-required-checkpoint.mjs`; it runs the REAL committed behavior-pinning tests (no reimplementation), maps each ADR-0046 verified-when clause to a named passing test, fails loudly on any red row or unmatched clause, and prints a green table exiting 0.

Close a governance-consistency gap required by the decisions README's HARD RULE ('no stale decisions'): a new ADR that amends existing ADRs must not land alone — the amended ADRs must carry a forward-pointer to it and a Status annotation. ADR-0046 (cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md) was created with an 'Amends: ADR-0013 and ADR-0023' line, but those two ADRs were NOT updated to point forward. Fix that now. Read `cocoder/decisions/README.md` (the 'Conventions' / HARD RULE section) and `cocoder/decisions/0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md` first.

FILES TO EDIT (exactly two): `cocoder/decisions/0013-orchestration-observation.md` and `cocoder/decisions/0023-workspace-commit-spine.md`.

FOR ADR-0013 (`0013-orchestration-observation.md`): in its Status/relationship header block (the lines just under the title, e.g. alongside **Status:** / **Refines:** / **Incorporates:**), add a forward-pointer line: `**Amended by:** [0046](./0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md) — for a code-touching atom, the per-atom verify gate now requires a GREEN run-tests result as a required input before commit; the deterministic 'no pass, no commit' boundary and Oscar's diff judgment are otherwise unchanged.` Do NOT rewrite the body or change the original decision text — this is a banner/pointer only (preserve the historical record, per the HARD RULE).

FOR ADR-0023 (`0023-workspace-commit-spine.md`): it already uses top-of-Decision amendment banners. Add an `**Amended by:** [0046](./0046-run-tests-required-checkpoint.md)` line to its Status/header block AND a short note that §3 (Verification matched to risk — the one verify gate) now takes a GREEN run-tests result as a REQUIRED INPUT for code-touching atoms — explicitly NOT a second gate or commit lane (the one-self-clearing-gate principle is preserved; run-tests feeds the single existing gate). Keep it a pointer/annotation; do NOT alter the existing §1-§6 decision text or the Amendment 1/2 banners.

MUST NOT: change any decision's actual ruling, renumber, edit any other ADR, or touch ADR-0046 itself. Both edits are pure additive forward-pointers preserving history.

EXIT CRITERION: these are governance/docs edits under cocoder/** (NOT code-touching), but they ARE behavior-pinned by the drift/doc-reference suites — run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (the full core suite, which includes drift-doc-reference-gate, drift-resolve-doc-references, drift-read-claims, and orchestration-contracts) and confirm it is GREEN; paste the command, exit code, and summary. The new `[0046](./0046-...)` links must resolve (the target file exists). Then `git status --porcelain` must show ONLY the two ADR files changed.

ACCEPTANCE: ADR-0013 and ADR-0023 each carry an additive `Amended by: ADR-0046` forward-pointer with a one-line description of the amendment; no decision body or ruling was altered; all doc-reference links resolve; the full core suite is green; only those two files changed.
